# Co-Scientist

Co-Scientist is an independent open-source implementation of a multi-agent workbench for scientific hypothesis generation, evidence review, debate, ranking, and synthesis.

The project is inspired by published Google DeepMind research. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or an official product of Google or Google DeepMind.

## Primary pages

- [Home](https://ai-co-scientist.com/): Product overview and workbench entry point.
- [Ferroptosis demo](https://ai-co-scientist.com/demos/ferroptosis-pancreatic-cancer): Completed seeded research demonstration.
- [Synaptic pruning demo](https://ai-co-scientist.com/demos/synaptic-pruning-cognitive-flexibility): Completed seeded research demonstration.
- [Biofilm resistance demo](https://ai-co-scientist.com/demos/biofilm-antibiotic-resistance): Completed seeded research demonstration.
- [Source code](https://github.com/guy915/Co-Scientist): Open-source repository.
- [Published research](https://research.google/blog/accelerating-scientific-breakthroughs-with-an-ai-co-scientist/): Background research from Google DeepMind.

## Notes

- Public demo pages summarize illustrative seeded runs.
- Workbench routes under `/runs` may contain user-created research and should not be indexed.
- Demonstration output is not medical or clinical guidance.
